  1726	    }
  1727	
  1728	    // T059: Archival Manager for automatic archival of ARCHIVED state memories
  1729	    try {
  1730	      archivalManager.init(database);
  1731	      // Start background archival job (scans every hour by default)
  1732	      archivalManager.startBackgroundJob();
  1733	      if (archivalManager.isBackgroundJobRunning()) {
  1734	        console.error(`[context-server] Archival manager initialized (background job started)`);

  1743	    // T099: Background retry job for pending embeddings (REQ-031, CHK-179)

  1746	      const retryJobResult = retryManager.startBackgroundJob({
  1747	        intervalMs: 5 * 60 * 1000,  // Check every 5 minutes
  1748	        batchSize: 5,               // Process up to 5 pending items per run
  1749	      });

  1751	        console.error('[context-server] Background retry job started (interval: 5min, batch: 5)');

  1759	
  1760	    // REQ-D4-006: Shadow feedback holdout evaluation background scheduler.

  1763	    try {
  1764	      const shadowSchedulerStarted = shadowEvaluationRuntime.startShadowEvaluationScheduler(database);
  1765	      if (shadowSchedulerStarted) {

  1767	      } else {

  1773	    }

  1775	    // REQ-D4-004: Batch feedback learning — runs one cycle at startup (shadow-only, no live ranking mutations).

  1777	    try {
  1778	      const batchResult = runBatchLearning(database);

     5	import fs from 'fs';
     6	import path from 'path';
     7	import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url';
     8	
     9	type ModuleLoader<TModule extends object> = () => Promise<TModule>;
    10	
    11	async function loadHandlerModule<TModule extends object>(moduleName: string): Promise<TModule> {
    12	  const basePath = path.join(import.meta.dirname, moduleName);
    13	  const candidatePaths = [`${basePath}.js`, `${basePath}.ts`, basePath];
    14	
    15	  for (const candidatePath of candidatePaths) {
    16	    if (fs.existsSync(candidatePath)) {
    17	      return await import(pathToFileURL(candidatePath).href) as TModule;
    18	    }
    19	  }
    20	
    21	  return await import(pathToFileURL(basePath).href) as TModule;
    22	}
    23	
    24	function lazyFunction<
    25	  TModule extends Record<string, unknown>,
    26	  TKey extends keyof TModule,
    27	>(load: ModuleLoader<TModule>, key: TKey): TModule[TKey] {
    28	  return (async (...args: unknown[]) => {
    29	    const module = await load();
    30	    const fn = module[key];
    31	    if (typeof fn !== 'function') {
    32	      throw new Error(`Lazy export '${String(key)}' is not callable`);
    33	    }
    34	    return await (fn as (...fnArgs: unknown[]) => unknown)(...args);
    35	  }) as TModule[TKey];
    36	}
    37	
    38	function lazyModule<TModule extends object>(load: ModuleLoader<TModule>): TModule {
    39	  return new Proxy({} as TModule, {
    40	    get(_target, property, receiver) {
    41	      return async (...args: unknown[]) => {
    42	        const module = await load();
    43	        const loadedValue = Reflect.get(module as object, property, receiver);
    44	        if (typeof loadedValue === 'function') {
    45	          return await (loadedValue as (...fnArgs: unknown[]) => unknown)(...args);
    46	        }
    47	        return loadedValue;
    48	      };
    49	    },
    50	    has(_target, property) {
    51	      return typeof property === 'string';
    52	    },
    53	    ownKeys() {
    54	      return [];
    55	    },
    56	    getOwnPropertyDescriptor(_target, _property) {
    57	      return {
    58	        configurable: true,
    59	        enumerable: true,
    60	      };
    61	    },
    62	  });
    63	}
    64

     9	} from './schemas/tool-input-schemas.js';

    11	/**
    12	 * Re-export schema validation helpers used by the MCP tool entry points.
    13	 */
    14	export {
    15	  ToolSchemaValidationError,
    16	  formatZodError,

    26	/**
    27	 * Normalized definition for a single MCP tool and its JSON schema.
    28	 */
    29	export interface ToolDefinition {
    30	  name: string;
    31	  description: string;
    32	  inputSchema: Record<string, unknown>;
    33	  outputSchema?: Record<string, unknown>;
    34	}
    35	
    36	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	// 2. TOOL DEFINITIONS

    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	// T061: L1 Orchestration - Unified entry point (Token Budget: 3500)
    41	const memoryContext: ToolDefinition = {
    42	  name: 'memory_context',
    43	  description: '[L1:Orchestration] Unified entry point for context retrieval with intent-aware routing. START HERE for most memory operations. For session recovery, use mode: \'resume\' with profile: \'resume\'. Automatically detects task intent (add_feature, fix_bug, refactor, security_audit, understand, find_spec, find_decision) and routes to optimal retrieval strategy. Modes: auto (default), quick (trigger-based), deep (comprehensive), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery). Token Budget: 3500. For code search by concept/intent, prefer mcp__cocoindex_code__search (CocoIndex). For structural code queries (callers, imports), prefer code_graph_query.',
    44	  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { input: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'The query, prompt, or context description (required)' }, mode: { type: 'string', enum: ['auto', 'quick', 'deep', 'focused', 'resume'], default: 'auto', description: 'Context retrieval mode: auto (detect intent), quick (fast triggers), deep (comprehensive search), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery)' }, intent: { type: 'string', enum: ['add_feature', 'fix_bug', 'refactor', 'security_audit', 'understand', 'find_spec', 'find_decision'], description: 'Explicit task intent. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },
    45	};
    46	
    47	// L2: Core - Primary operations (Token Budget: 3500)
    48	const memorySearch: ToolDefinition = {
    49	  name: 'memory_search',
    50	  description: '[L2:Core] Search conversation memories semantically using vector similarity. Returns ranked results with similarity scores. Constitutional tier memories are ALWAYS included at the top of results (~2000 tokens max), regardless of query. Requires query (string), concepts (array of 2-5 strings), or cursor (string) for continuation pagination. Supports intent-aware retrieval (REQ-006) with task-specific weight adjustments. When implicit feedback logging is enabled, searches also emit shadow-only feedback signals such as search_shown and, for includeContent runs, result_cited. Token Budget: 3500. For code search by concept/intent, prefer mcp__cocoindex_code__search (CocoIndex). For structural code queries (callers, imports), prefer code_graph_query.',

    33	function hasValue(value: unknown): boolean {

    52	}

    54	function validateType(field: string, value: unknown, expectedType: string): void {

    57	  }
    58	
    59	  if (expectedType === 'number' && !isNumericLike(value)) {

    79	function validateProperty(field: string, value: unknown, schema: JsonSchemaProperty): void {

   124	    }

   127	    }

   129	}
   130	
   131	function matchesSchemaConstraint(args: Record<string, unknown>, constraint: SchemaConstraint): boolean {
   132	  const required = constraint.required ?? [];
   133	  if (required.some((field) => !hasValue(args[field]))) {
   134	    return false;
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  if (constraint.properties) {

   142	      }

   145	      } catch {

   147	      }
   148	    }
   149	  }
   150	
   151	  if (Array.isArray(constraint.allOf) && constraint.allOf.length > 0) {
   152	    if (!constraint.allOf.every((entry) => matchesSchemaConstraint(args, entry))) {
   153	      return false;
   154	    }
   155	  }
   156	
   157	  if (Array.isArray(constraint.anyOf) && constraint.anyOf.length > 0) {
   158	    if (!constraint.anyOf.some((entry) => matchesSchemaConstraint(args, entry))) {
   159	      return false;
   160	    }
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  if (Array.isArray(constraint.oneOf) && constraint.oneOf.length > 0) {
   164	    const matchedCount = constraint.oneOf.filter((entry) => matchesSchemaConstraint(args, entry)).length;
   165	    if (matchedCount !== 1) {
   166	      return false;
   167	    }
   168	  }
   169	
   170	  return true;
   171	}
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * Validate tool arguments against the declared MCP input schema.
   175	 *
   176	 * Coverage is intentionally focused on compatibility-safe checks:
   177	 * - required fields
   178	 * - oneOf constraint variants
   179	 * - basic types / enums / const / min / max for provided fields
   180	 *
   181	 * Unknown tools or missing schemas are skipped to preserve legacy behavior.
   182	 */
   183	export function validateToolInputSchema(
   184	  toolName: string,
   185	  args: Record<string, unknown>,
   186	  toolDefinitions: ToolDefinition[]
   187	): void {
   188	  const toolDefinition = toolDefinitions.find((tool) => tool.name === toolName);
   189	  if (!toolDefinition) {
   190	    return;
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  const schema = toolDefinition.inputSchema as InputSchema;
   194	  if (schema.type === 'object' && (args === null || typeof args !== 'object' || Array.isArray(args))) {
   195	    throw new Error(`Invalid arguments for '${toolName}': expected object`);
   196	  }
   197	
   198	  const required = schema.required ?? [];
   199	  const missing = required.filter((field) => !hasValue(args[field]));
   200	  if (missing.length > 0) {
   201	    throw new Error(`Missing required arguments for '${toolName}': ${missing.join(', ')}`);
   202	  }
   203	
   204	  if (Array.isArray(schema['x-requiredAnyOf']) && schema['x-requiredAnyOf'].length > 0) {
   205	    const branchMatched = schema['x-requiredAnyOf'].some((fields) =>
   206	      Array.isArray(fields) && fields.every((field) => hasValue(args[field]))
   207	    );
   208	    if (!branchMatched) {
   209	      throw new Error(`Arguments for '${toolName}' do not satisfy required schema constraints`);
   210	    }
   211	  }
   212	
   213	  if (!schema.properties) {
   214	    if (!matchesSchemaConstraint(args, schema)) {
   215	      throw new Error(`Arguments for '${toolName}' do not satisfy required schema constraints`);
   216	    }
   217	    return;
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(args)) {
   221	    if (schema.additionalProperties === false && !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(schema.properties, field)) {
   222	      throw new Error(`Unknown argument for '${toolName}': ${field}`);
   223	    }
   224	    if (!hasValue(value)) {
   225	      continue;
   226	    }
   227	    const propertySchema = schema.properties[field];
   228	    if (!propertySchema) {
   229	      continue;
   230	    }
   231	    validateProperty(field, value, propertySchema);
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  if (!matchesSchemaConstraint(args, schema)) {
   235	    throw new Error(`Arguments for '${toolName}' do not satisfy required schema constraints`);
   236	  }
   237	}

The contract looks weaker than the packet implies, so I’m checking one more layer before I call anything: whether these coverage-graph handlers are actually wired into the MCP dispatch path at all, and whether the published schemas have matching runtime validators. That’s the spot where a “looks correct on paper” security surface often breaks.

  1243	      console.error('[context-server] Run memory_index_scan manually after model loads');
  1244	      return;
  1245	    }
  1246	
  1247	    // T107: Recover any pending files from previous failed index operations
  1248	    await recoverPendingFiles(basePath);

  1250	    console.error('[context-server] Starting background scan for new memory files...');
  1251	    const scanRoots = Array.from(
  1252	      new Set(
  1253	        [basePath, ...ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS]
  1254	          .filter((root): root is string => typeof root === 'string' && root.trim().length > 0)
  1255	          .map((root) => path.resolve(root))
  1256	      )
  1257	    );
  1258	    const allFiles: string[] = [];
  1259	    const seen = new Set<string>();
  1260	
  1261	    for (const root of scanRoots) {
  1262	      try {
  1263	        const rootFiles = [
  1264	          ...memoryParser.findMemoryFiles(root),
  1265	          ...memoryIndexDiscovery.findConstitutionalFiles(root),
  1266	          ...memoryIndexDiscovery.findSpecDocuments(root),
  1267	        ];
  1268	
  1269	        for (const filePath of rootFiles) {
  1270	          const resolved = path.resolve(filePath);
  1271	          if (!seen.has(resolved)) {
  1272	            seen.add(resolved);
  1273	            allFiles.push(filePath);
  1274	          }
  1275	        }
  1276	      } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1277	        // Non-fatal: skip inaccessible startup roots.
  1278	      }
  1279	    }
  1280	
  1281	    if (allFiles.length === 0) {
  1282	      console.error('[context-server] No memory files found in workspace');
  1283	      return;
  1284	    }
  1285	
  1286	    console.error(`[context-server] Found ${allFiles.length} memory files, checking for changes...`);
  1287	    let indexed = 0, updated = 0, unchanged = 0, failed = 0;
  1288	
  1289	    for (const filePath of allFiles) {
  1290	      try {
  1291	        const result: IndexResult = await indexSingleFile(filePath, false);
  1292	        if (result.status === 'indexed') indexed++;
  1293	        else if (result.status === 'updated') updated++;
  1294	        else unchanged++;
  1295	      } catch (error: unknown) {
  1296	        failed++;
  1297	        const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1298	        console.error(`[context-server] Failed to index ${path.basename(filePath)}: ${message}`);
  1299	      }
  1300	    }
  1301	
  1302	    if (indexed > 0 || updated > 0) {
  1303	      console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan: ${indexed} new, ${updated} updated, ${unchanged} unchanged, ${failed} failed`);
  1304	    } else {
  1305	      console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan: all ${unchanged} files up to date`);
  1306	    }
  1307	
  1308	    if (indexed > 0 || updated > 0) {
  1309	      try {
  1310	        runPostMutationHooks('scan', {
  1311	          indexed,
  1312	          updated,
  1313	          staleDeleted: 0,
  1314	          staleDeleteFailed: 0,
  1315	          operation: 'startup-scan',
  1316	        });
  1317	      } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1318	        // Non-fatal: startup scan must continue even if invalidation hooks fail.
  1319	      }
  1320	    }
  1321	
  1322	    // Log atomicity metrics for monitoring (CHK-190)
  1323	    const metrics = transactionManager.getMetrics();
  1324	    if (metrics.totalRecoveries > 0 || metrics.totalErrors > 0) {
  1325	      console.error(`[context-server] Atomicity metrics: ${metrics.totalAtomicWrites} successful, ${metrics.totalErrors} failed, ${metrics.totalRecoveries} recovered`);
  1326	    }
  1327	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1328	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1329	    console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan error: ${message}`);
  1330	  } finally {
  1331	    startupScanInProgress = false;
  1332	  }
  1333	}
  1334	
  1335	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1336	   7. GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN
  1337	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  1338	
  1339	let shuttingDown = false;
  1340	// P1-09 FIX: Hoist transport to module scope so shutdown handlers can close it
  1341	let transport: StdioServerTransport | null = null;
  1342	// P1-11 FIX: Module-level guard to avoid redundant initializeDb() calls per tool invocation
  1343	let dbInitialized = false;
  1344	let fileWatcher: FSWatcher | null = null;
  1345	
  1346	/** Maximum time (ms) to wait for async cleanup before force-exiting. */
  1347	const SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS = 5000;
  1348	
  1349	export const __testables = {
  1350	  runCleanupStep,
  1351	  runAsyncCleanupStep,
  1352	  main: () => main(),
  1353	  normalizeGraphFilePath,
  1354	  extractFilePathsFromToolArgs,
  1355	};
  1356	
  1357	async function fatalShutdown(reason: string, exitCode: number): Promise<void> {
  1358	  if (shuttingDown) return;
  1359	  shuttingDown = true;
  1360	  console.error(`[context-server] ${reason}`);
  1361	
  1362	  runCleanupStep('sessionManager', () => sessionManager.shutdown());
  1363	  runCleanupStep('archivalManager', () => archivalManager.cleanup());
  1364	  runCleanupStep('retryManager', () => retryManager.stopBackgroundJob());
  1365	  runCleanupStep('shadowEvaluationRuntime', () => shadowEvaluationRuntime.stopShadowEvaluationScheduler());
  1366	  runCleanupStep('accessTracker', () => accessTracker.reset());
  1367	  runCleanupStep('toolCache', () => toolCache.shutdown());
  1368	
  1369	  let deadlineTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
  1370	  const cleanup = (async () => {
  1371	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('fileWatcher', async () => {
  1372	      if (fileWatcher) {
  1373	        await fileWatcher.close();
  1374	        fileWatcher = null;
  1375	      }
  1376	    });
  1377	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('local-reranker', async () => {
  1378	      await disposeLocalReranker();
  1379	    });
  1380	    runCleanupStep('vectorIndex', () => vectorIndex.closeDb());
  1381	    // P1-09 FIX: Close MCP transport on shutdown
  1382	    runCleanupStep('transport', () => {
  1383	      if (transport) {
  1384	        transport.close();
  1385	        transport = null;
  1386	      }
  1387	    });
  1388	  })();
  1389	
  1390	  const timedOut = await Promise.race([
  1391	    cleanup.then(() => false),
  1392	    new Promise<boolean>((resolve) => {
  1393	      deadlineTimer = setTimeout(() => resolve(true), SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS);
  1394	    }),
  1395	  ]);
  1396	
  1397	  if (deadlineTimer) {
  1398	    clearTimeout(deadlineTimer);
  1399	  }
  1400	
  1401	  if (timedOut) {
  1402	    console.error(`[context-server] Shutdown deadline exceeded after ${SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS}ms`);
  1403	  }
  1404	
  1405	  process.exit(exitCode);
  1406	}
  1407	
  1408	/** Remove indexed rows for watcher delete and rename events. */
  1409	async function removeIndexedMemoriesForFile(filePath: string): Promise<void> {
  1410	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
  1411	  if (!database) {
  1412	    return;
  1413	  }
  1414	
  1415	  const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
  1416	  let rows: Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }> = [];
  1417	
  1418	  try {
  1419	    rows = database.prepare(`
  1420	      SELECT id, spec_folder
  1421	      FROM memory_index
  1422	      WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
  1423	      ORDER BY id ASC
  1424	    `).all(canonicalPath, filePath) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }>;
  1425	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1426	    rows = database.prepare(`
  1427	      SELECT id, spec_folder
  1428	      FROM memory_index
  1429	      WHERE file_path = ?
  1430	      ORDER BY id ASC
  1431	    `).all(filePath) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }>;
  1432	  }
  1433	
  1434	  let deletedCount = 0;
  1435	  for (const row of rows) {
  1436	    if (typeof row.id === 'number') {
  1437	      if (vectorIndex.deleteMemory(row.id)) {
  1438	        deletedCount += 1;
  1439	        // Record DELETE history only after confirmed deletion.
  1440	        try {
  1441	          recordHistory(row.id, 'DELETE', filePath ?? null, null, 'mcp:file_watcher', row.spec_folder ?? null);
  1442	        } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
  1443	          // History recording is best-effort in file-watcher path
  1444	        }
  1445	      }
  1446	    }
  1447	  }
  1448	
  1449	  if (deletedCount > 0) {
  1450	    try {
  1451	      runPostMutationHooks('delete', { filePath, deletedCount });
  1452	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1453	      // Non-throwing by design: file-watcher path must not crash the server.
  1454	    }
  1455	  }
  1456	}
  1457	
  1458	process.on('SIGTERM', () => {
  1459	  void fatalShutdown('Received SIGTERM, shutting down...', 0);
  1460	});
  1461	process.on('SIGINT', () => {
  1462	  void fatalShutdown('Received SIGINT, shutting down...', 0);
  1463	});
  1464	
  1465	process.on('uncaughtException', (err: Error) => {
  1466	  void fatalShutdown(`Uncaught exception: ${err.stack ?? err.message}`, 1);
  1467	});
  1468	
  1469	process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason: unknown, promise: Promise<unknown>) => {
  1470	  void fatalShutdown(
  1471	    `Unhandled rejection at: ${String(promise)} reason: ${reason instanceof Error ? reason.stack ?? reason.message : String(reason)}`,
  1472	    1,
  1473	  );
  1474	});
  1475	
  1476	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1477	   8. MAIN
  1478	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  1479	
  1480	async function main(): Promise<void> {
  1481	  // Node version mismatch detection (non-blocking)
  1482	  detectNodeVersionMismatch();
  1483	  detectedRuntime = detectRuntime();
  1484	  console.error(
  1485	    `[context-server] Detected runtime: ${detectedRuntime.runtime} (hookPolicy=${detectedRuntime.hookPolicy})`,
  1486	  );
  1487	
  1488	  validateConfiguredEmbeddingsProvider();
  1489	
  1490	  // T087-T090: Pre-Flight API Key Validation (REQ-029)
  1491	  // Validates API key at startup to fail fast with actionable error messages
  1492	  // Skip validation if SPECKIT_SKIP_API_VALIDATION=true (for testing/CI)
  1493	  let startupEmbeddingConfig: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig>> | null = null;
  1494	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_SKIP_API_VALIDATION !== 'true') {
  1495	    console.error('[context-server] Validating embedding API key...');
  1496	    try {
  1497	      startupEmbeddingConfig = await resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig({ timeout: API_KEY_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT_MS });
  1498	      const validation: ApiKeyValidation = startupEmbeddingConfig.validation;
  1499	
  1500	      if (!validation.valid) {
  1501	        if (validation.networkError) {
  1502	          // Transient network failure — warn and continue, validation will occur on first use
  1503	          console.warn('[context-server] ===== API KEY VALIDATION SKIPPED (network error) =====');
  1504	          console.warn(`[context-server] Provider: ${validation.provider}`);
  1505	          console.warn(`[context-server] Error: ${validation.error}`);

  1140	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1141	   6. STARTUP SCAN & PENDING FILE RECOVERY
  1142	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  1143	
  1144	let startupScanInProgress = false;
  1145	
  1146	function getStartupWorkspaceRoots(basePath: string): string[] {
  1147	  const configuredMemoryRoot = process.env.MEMORY_BASE_PATH?.trim();
  1148	  const derivedMemoryRoot = configuredMemoryRoot ? path.resolve(process.cwd(), configuredMemoryRoot) : null;
  1149	  return Array.from(new Set(
  1150	    [basePath, derivedMemoryRoot, ...ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS]
  1151	      .filter((root): root is string => typeof root === 'string' && root.trim().length > 0)
  1152	      .map((root) => path.resolve(root))
  1153	  ));
  1154	}
  1155	
  1156	function getPendingRecoveryLocations(basePath: string): string[] {
  1157	  const scanLocations: string[] = [];
  1158	  for (const root of getStartupWorkspaceRoots(basePath)) {
  1159	    scanLocations.push(path.join(root, 'specs'));
  1160	    scanLocations.push(path.join(root, '.opencode', 'specs'));
  1161	    const skillDir = path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skill');
  1162	    try {
  1163	      if (!fs.existsSync(skillDir)) continue;
  1164	      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(skillDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
  1165	        if (!entry.isDirectory() || entry.name.startsWith('.')) continue;
  1166	        const constDir = path.join(skillDir, entry.name, 'constitutional');
  1167	        if (fs.existsSync(constDir)) scanLocations.push(constDir);
  1168	      }
  1169	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1170	      // Non-fatal: constitutional directory discovery failed
  1171	    }
  1172	  }
  1173	  return Array.from(new Set(scanLocations.filter((location) => fs.existsSync(location))));
  1174	}
  1175	
  1176	/**
  1177	 * T107: Recover pending memory files on MCP startup.
  1178	 * CHK-188: Pending files processed by recovery job on next startup.
  1179	 *
  1180	 * Scans for files with _pending suffix (created when index failed after file write)
  1181	 * and attempts to index them.
  1182	 */
  1183	async function recoverPendingFiles(basePath: string): Promise<PendingRecoveryResult> {
  1184	  console.error('[context-server] Checking for pending memory files...');
  1185	
  1186	  try {
  1187	    // BUG-028 FIX: Restrict scan to known memory file locations to prevent OOM when scanning large workspaces.
  1188	    // P1-002-SCAN: share the same allowed-root expansion that startup indexing uses.
  1189	    const existingScanLocations = getPendingRecoveryLocations(basePath);
  1190	
  1191	    // P1 FIX: Wire isCommittedInDb callback so stale pending files are detected at startup.
  1192	    // VectorIndex.getDb() is an initializing accessor — always returns a valid DB after initializeDb().
  1193	    const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
  1194	    const isCommittedInDb = (originalPath: string): boolean => {
  1195	      const row = (database.prepare('SELECT 1 FROM memory_index WHERE file_path = ?') as import('better-sqlite3').Statement).get(originalPath);
  1196	      return !!row;
  1197	    };
  1198	
  1199	    const rawResults = existingScanLocations.flatMap((location) =>
  1200	      transactionManager.recoverAllPendingFiles(location, isCommittedInDb)
  1201	    );
  1202	
  1203	    // Aggregate per-file results into a summary
  1204	    const found = rawResults.length;
  1205	    const recovered = rawResults.filter(r => r.recovered).length;
  1206	    const failed = found - recovered;
  1207	
  1208	    const recoveryResult: PendingRecoveryResult = {
  1209	      found,
  1210	      processed: found,
  1211	      recovered,
  1212	      failed,
  1213	      results: rawResults,
  1214	    };
  1215	
  1216	    if (recoveryResult.found > 0) {
  1217	      console.error(`[context-server] Pending file recovery: ${recoveryResult.recovered} recovered, ${recoveryResult.failed} failed (${recoveryResult.found} total)`);
  1218	    } else {
  1219	      console.error('[context-server] No pending memory files found');
  1220	    }
  1221	
  1222	    return recoveryResult;
  1223	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1224	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1225	    console.error(`[context-server] Pending file recovery error: ${message}`);
  1226	    return { found: 0, processed: 0, recovered: 0, failed: 0, results: [] };
  1227	  }
  1228	}
  1229	
  1230	async function startupScan(basePath: string): Promise<void> {
  1231	  if (startupScanInProgress) {
  1232	    console.error('[context-server] Startup scan already in progress, skipping');
  1233	    return;
  1234	  }
  1235	
  1236	  startupScanInProgress = true;
  1237	  try {
  1238	    console.error('[context-server] Waiting for embedding model to be ready...');
  1239	    const modelReady: boolean = await waitForEmbeddingModel(EMBEDDING_MODEL_TIMEOUT_MS);
  1240

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Index
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Re-exports all tool dispatch modules for context-server (T303).
     5	import * as contextTools from './context-tools.js';
     6	import * as memoryTools from './memory-tools.js';
     7	import * as causalTools from './causal-tools.js';
     8	import * as checkpointTools from './checkpoint-tools.js';
     9	import * as lifecycleTools from './lifecycle-tools.js';
    10	import * as codeGraphTools from './code-graph-tools.js';
    11	
    12	export { contextTools, memoryTools, causalTools, checkpointTools, lifecycleTools, codeGraphTools };
    13	
    14	export type { MCPResponse } from './types.js';
    15	
    16	/** All tool dispatch modules in priority order */
    17	export const ALL_DISPATCHERS = [
    18	  contextTools,
    19	  memoryTools,
    20	  causalTools,
    21	  checkpointTools,
    22	  lifecycleTools,
    23	  codeGraphTools,
    24	] as const;
    25	
    26	/** Dispatch a tool call to the appropriate module. Returns null if unrecognized. */
    27	export async function dispatchTool(
    28	  name: string,
    29	  args: Record<string, unknown>,
    30	): Promise<import('./types.js').MCPResponse | null> {
    31	  for (const dispatcher of ALL_DISPATCHERS) {
    32	    if (dispatcher.TOOL_NAMES.has(name)) {
    33	      return dispatcher.handleTool(name, args);
    34	    }
    35	  }
    36	  return null;
    37	}

     9	  handleMemoryIngestStatus,
    10	  handleMemoryIngestCancel,

    12	  handleTaskPostflight,
    13	  handleGetLearningHistory,
    14	  handleEvalRunAblation,

    16	  handleSessionHealth,
    17	  handleSessionResume,
    18	  handleSessionBootstrap,
    19	} from '../handlers/index.js';
    20	import { validateToolArgs } from '../schemas/tool-input-schemas.js';
    21	
    22	import { parseArgs } from './types.js';
    23	import type {
    24	  MCPResponse,
    25	  ScanArgs,
    26	  PreflightArgs,
    27	  PostflightArgs,
    28	  LearningHistoryArgs,
    29	  EvalRunAblationArgs,
    30	  EvalReportingDashboardArgs,
    31	  IngestStartArgs,
    32	  IngestStatusArgs,
    33	  IngestCancelArgs,
    34	  SessionResumeArgs,
    35	  SessionBootstrapArgs,
    36	} from './types.js';
    37	
    38	/** Tool names handled by this module */
    39	export const TOOL_NAMES = new Set([
    40	  'memory_index_scan',
    41	  'task_preflight',
    42	  'task_postflight',
    43	  'memory_get_learning_history',
    44	  'memory_ingest_start',
    45	  'memory_ingest_status',
    46	  'memory_ingest_cancel',
    47	  'eval_run_ablation',
    48	  'eval_reporting_dashboard',
    49	  'session_health',
    50	  'session_resume',
    51	  'session_bootstrap',
    52	]);
    53	
    54	/** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
    55	export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
    56	  switch (name) {
    57	    case 'memory_index_scan':          return handleMemoryIndexScan(parseArgs<ScanArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_index_scan', args)));
    58	    case 'task_preflight':             return handleTaskPreflight(parseArgs<PreflightArgs>(validateToolArgs('task_preflight', args)));
    59	    case 'task_postflight':            return handleTaskPostflight(parseArgs<PostflightArgs>(validateToolArgs('task_postflight', args)));
    60	    case 'memory_get_learning_history': return handleGetLearningHistory(parseArgs<LearningHistoryArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_get_learning_history', args)));
    61	    case 'memory_ingest_start':        return handleMemoryIngestStart(parseArgs<IngestStartArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_ingest_start', args)));
    62	    case 'memory_ingest_status':       return handleMemoryIngestStatus(parseArgs<IngestStatusArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_ingest_status', args)));
    63	    case 'memory_ingest_cancel':       return handleMemoryIngestCancel(parseArgs<IngestCancelArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_ingest_cancel', args)));
    64	    case 'eval_run_ablation':          return handleEvalRunAblation(parseArgs<EvalRunAblationArgs>(validateToolArgs('eval_run_ablation', args)));
    65	    case 'eval_reporting_dashboard':   return handleEvalReportingDashboard(parseArgs<EvalReportingDashboardArgs>(validateToolArgs('eval_reporting_dashboard', args)));
    66	    case 'session_health':             return handleSessionHealth();
    67	    case 'session_resume':             return handleSessionResume(parseArgs<SessionResumeArgs>(validateToolArgs('session_resume', args)));
    68	    case 'session_bootstrap':          return handleSessionBootstrap(parseArgs<SessionBootstrapArgs>(validateToolArgs('session_bootstrap', args)));
    69	    default: return null;
    70	  }
    71	}

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Code Graph Tools
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Dispatch for code graph MCP tools: scan, query, status, context.
     5	import {
     6	  handleCodeGraphScan,
     7	  handleCodeGraphQuery,
     8	  handleCodeGraphStatus,
     9	  handleCodeGraphContext,
    10	  handleCccStatus,
    11	  handleCccReindex,
    12	  handleCccFeedback,
    13	} from '../handlers/code-graph/index.js';
    14	
    15	import type { MCPResponse } from './types.js';
    16	
    17	/** Tool names handled by this module */
    18	export const TOOL_NAMES = new Set([
    19	  'code_graph_scan',
    20	  'code_graph_query',
    21	  'code_graph_status',
    22	  'code_graph_context',
    23	  'ccc_status',
    24	  'ccc_reindex',
    25	  'ccc_feedback',
    26	]);
    27	
    28	/** Coerce handler response to MCPResponse (fix type literal narrowing) */
    29	function toMCP(result: { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }): MCPResponse {
    30	  return {
    31	    content: result.content.map(c => ({ type: 'text' as const, text: c.text })),
    32	  };
    33	}
    34	
    35	function getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args: Record<string, unknown>, requiredKeys: string[]): string[] {
    36	  return requiredKeys.filter((key) => {
    37	    const value = args[key];
    38	    return typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0;
    39	  });
    40	}
    41	
    42	function validationError(tool: string, missingKeys: string[]): MCPResponse {
    43	  return {
    44	    content: [{
    45	      type: 'text',
    46	      text: JSON.stringify({
    47	        status: 'error',
    48	        error: `Missing required field${missingKeys.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}: ${missingKeys.join(', ')}`,
    49	        tool,
    50	      }),
    51	    }],
    52	  };
    53	}
    54	
    55	/** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
    56	export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
    57	  switch (name) {
    58	    case 'code_graph_scan':
    59	      return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphScan(args as Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphScan>[0]));
    60	    case 'code_graph_query': {
    61	      const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['operation', 'subject']);
    62	      if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
    63	        return validationError(name, missingKeys);
    64	      }
    65	      return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphQuery(args as unknown as Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphQuery>[0]));
    66	    }
    67	    case 'code_graph_status':
    68	      return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphStatus());
    69	    case 'code_graph_context':
    70	      return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphContext(args as Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphContext>[0]));
    71	    case 'ccc_status':
    72	      return toMCP(await handleCccStatus());
    73	    case 'ccc_reindex':
    74	      return toMCP(await handleCccReindex(args as Parameters<typeof handleCccReindex>[0]));
    75	    case 'ccc_feedback': {
    76	      const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['query', 'rating']);
    77	      if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
    78	        return validationError(name, missingKeys);
    79	      }
    80	      return toMCP(await handleCccFeedback(args as unknown as Parameters<typeof handleCccFeedback>[0]));
    81	    }
    82	    default:
    83	      return null;
    84	  }
    85	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Memory Tools
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Dispatch for L2-L4 memory tools: search, triggers, save,
     5	// List, stats, health, delete, update, validate (T303).
     6	import {
     7	  handleMemorySearch,
     8	  handleMemoryMatchTriggers,
     9	  handleMemorySave,
    10	  handleMemoryList,
    11	  handleMemoryStats,
    12	  handleMemoryHealth,
    13	  handleMemoryDelete,
    14	  handleMemoryUpdate,
    15	  handleMemoryValidate,
    16	  handleMemoryBulkDelete,
    17	} from '../handlers/index.js';
    18	import { validateToolArgs } from '../schemas/tool-input-schemas.js';
    19	
    20	import { parseArgs } from './types.js';
    21	import type {
    22	  MCPResponse,
    23	  SearchArgs,
    24	  TriggerArgs,
    25	  SaveArgs,
    26	  ListArgs,
    27	  StatsArgs,
    28	  HealthArgs,
    29	  DeleteArgs,
    30	  UpdateArgs,
    31	  MemoryValidateArgs,
    32	  BulkDeleteArgs,
    33	} from './types.js';
    34	
    35	function relabelResponseTool(response: MCPResponse, toolName: string): MCPResponse {
    36	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
    37	  if (!firstEntry || firstEntry.type !== 'text' || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
    38	    return response;
    39	  }
    40	
    41	  try {
    42	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
    43	    const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object'
    44	      ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
    45	      : {};
    46	    envelope.meta = {
    47	      ...meta,
    48	      tool: toolName,
    49	    };
    50	
    51	    return {
    52	      ...response,
    53	      content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],
    54	    };
    55	  } catch {
    56	    return response;
    57	  }
    58	}
    59	
    60	/** Tool names handled by this module */
    61	export const TOOL_NAMES = new Set([
    62	  'memory_search',
    63	  'memory_quick_search',
    64	  'memory_match_triggers',
    65	  'memory_save',
    66	  'memory_list',
    67	  'memory_stats',
    68	  'memory_health',
    69	  'memory_delete',
    70	  'memory_update',
    71	  'memory_validate',
    72	  'memory_bulk_delete',
    73	]);
    74	
    75	/** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
    76	export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
    77	  switch (name) {
    78	    case 'memory_search':         return handleMemorySearch(parseArgs<SearchArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_search', args)));
    79	    case 'memory_quick_search': {
    80	      // E3: Delegate to memory_search with sensible defaults
    81	      const validated = validateToolArgs('memory_quick_search', args);
    82	      const quickArgs: SearchArgs = {
    83	        query: validated.query as string,
    84	        limit: typeof validated.limit === 'number' ? validated.limit : 10,
    85	        specFolder: validated.specFolder as string | undefined,
    86	        tenantId: validated.tenantId as string | undefined,
    87	        userId: validated.userId as string | undefined,
    88	        agentId: validated.agentId as string | undefined,
    89	        autoDetectIntent: true,
    90	        enableDedup: true,
    91	        includeContent: true,
    92	        includeConstitutional: true,
    93	        rerank: true,
    94	      };
    95	      const response = await handleMemorySearch(quickArgs);
    96	      return relabelResponseTool(response, 'memory_quick_search');
    97	    }
    98	    case 'memory_match_triggers': return handleMemoryMatchTriggers(parseArgs<TriggerArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_match_triggers', args)));
    99	    case 'memory_save':           return handleMemorySave(parseArgs<SaveArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_save', args)));
   100	    case 'memory_list':           return handleMemoryList(parseArgs<ListArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_list', args)));
   101	    case 'memory_stats':          return handleMemoryStats(parseArgs<StatsArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_stats', args)));
   102	    case 'memory_health':         return handleMemoryHealth(parseArgs<HealthArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_health', args)));
   103	    case 'memory_delete':         return handleMemoryDelete(parseArgs<DeleteArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_delete', args)));
   104	    case 'memory_update':         return handleMemoryUpdate(parseArgs<UpdateArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_update', args)));
   105	    case 'memory_validate':       return handleMemoryValidate(parseArgs<MemoryValidateArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_validate', args)));
   106	    case 'memory_bulk_delete':    return handleMemoryBulkDelete(parseArgs<BulkDeleteArgs>(validateToolArgs('memory_bulk_delete', args)));
   107	    default: return null;
   108	  }
   109	}

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/coverage-graph-db.vitest.ts | sed -n '1,340p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// COVERAGE GRAPH DB INTEGRATION TESTS (T022)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Tests for the coverage graph database projection contract.
     5	// Validates upsert, query, replay, and latency expectations
     6	// for the MCP-maintained SQLite graph projection.
     7	
     8	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
     9	
    10	/**
    11	 * Coverage Graph DB contract test stubs.
    12	 *
    13	 * These tests validate the contract defined in state_format.md
    14	 * Section 12 (Coverage Graph Integration Contract). The actual
    15	 * MCP implementation will be wired in Phase 3; these tests
    16	 * verify the contract shape and expected behaviors.
    17	 */
    18	
    19	// --- Contract shape validation ---
    20	
    21	interface UpsertPayload {
    22	  namespace: string;
    23	  nodes: Array<{ id: string; type: string; label: string; iteration: number }>;
    24	  edges: Array<{ source: string; target: string; relation: string; weight: number; iteration: number }>;
    25	  iteration: number;
    26	}
    27	
    28	interface UpsertResponse {
    29	  accepted: number;
    30	  rejected: number;
    31	  signals: {
    32	    componentCount: number;
    33	    largestComponentSize: number;
    34	    isolatedNodes: number;
    35	    totalNodes: number;
    36	    totalEdges: number;
    37	  };
    38	}
    39	
    40	describe('coverage-graph-db contract', () => {
    41	  describe('upsert payload shape', () => {
    42	    it('validates a well-formed upsert payload', () => {
    43	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    44	        namespace: 'dr-2026-03-18T10-00-00Z',
    45	        nodes: [
    46	          { id: 'q-1', type: 'question_node', label: 'What causes latency?', iteration: 3 },
    47	          { id: 'f-2', type: 'finding_node', label: 'Connection pooling bottleneck', iteration: 3 },
    48	        ],
    49	        edges: [
    50	          { source: 'f-2', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS', weight: 1.3, iteration: 3 },
    51	        ],
    52	        iteration: 3,
    53	      };
    54	
    55	      expect(payload.namespace).toBeTruthy();
    56	      expect(payload.nodes.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    57	      expect(payload.edges.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    58	      expect(payload.iteration).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    59	    });
    60	
    61	    it('requires namespace on every payload', () => {
    62	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    63	        namespace: '',
    64	        nodes: [],
    65	        edges: [],
    66	        iteration: 1,
    67	      };
    68	
    69	      // Empty namespace should be rejected
    70	      expect(payload.namespace).toBeFalsy();
    71	    });
    72	
    73	    it('accepts empty nodes and edges arrays', () => {
    74	      const payload: UpsertPayload = {
    75	        namespace: 'test-ns',
    76	        nodes: [],
    77	        edges: [],
    78	        iteration: 1,
    79	      };
    80	
    81	      expect(payload.nodes.length).toBe(0);
    82	      expect(payload.edges.length).toBe(0);
    83	    });
    84	  });
    85	
    86	  describe('upsert response shape', () => {
    87	    it('validates a well-formed response', () => {
    88	      const response: UpsertResponse = {
    89	        accepted: 5,
    90	        rejected: 0,
    91	        signals: {
    92	          componentCount: 2,
    93	          largestComponentSize: 8,
    94	          isolatedNodes: 1,
    95	          totalNodes: 10,
    96	          totalEdges: 12,
    97	        },
    98	      };
    99	
   100	      expect(response.accepted).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   101	      expect(response.rejected).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   102	      expect(response.accepted + response.rejected).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   103	      expect(response.signals.componentCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   104	      expect(response.signals.totalNodes).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
   105	    });
   106	
   107	    it('signals contain all required fields', () => {
   108	      const signals: UpsertResponse['signals'] = {
   109	        componentCount: 1,
   110	        largestComponentSize: 5,
   111	        isolatedNodes: 0,
   112	        totalNodes: 5,
   113	        totalEdges: 4,
   114	      };
   115	
   116	      const requiredKeys = ['componentCount', 'largestComponentSize', 'isolatedNodes', 'totalNodes', 'totalEdges'];
   117	      for (const key of requiredKeys) {
   118	        expect(signals).toHaveProperty(key);
   119	      }
   120	    });
   121	  });
   122	
   123	  describe('latency budget contract', () => {
   124	    it('defines 500ms budget for upsert batches', () => {
   125	      const UPSERT_BUDGET_MS = 500;
   126	      expect(UPSERT_BUDGET_MS).toBe(500);
   127	    });
   128	
   129	    it('defines 200ms budget for queries', () => {
   130	      const QUERY_BUDGET_MS = 200;
   131	      expect(QUERY_BUDGET_MS).toBe(200);
   132	    });
   133	  });
   134	
   135	  describe('replay semantics', () => {
   136	    it('graph is rebuildable from JSONL alone', () => {
   137	      // Simulate replay: collect all graphEvents from iteration records
   138	      const iterationRecords = [
   139	        {
   140	          type: 'iteration',
   141	          run: 1,
   142	          graphEvents: [
   143	            { kind: 'node', id: 'q-1', nodeType: 'question_node', label: 'Q1' },
   144	            { kind: 'edge', source: 'f-1', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS' },
   145	          ],
   146	        },
   147	        {
   148	          type: 'iteration',
   149	          run: 2,
   150	          graphEvents: [
   151	            { kind: 'node', id: 'f-2', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'F2' },
   152	            { kind: 'edge', source: 'f-2', target: 'q-1', relation: 'ANSWERS' },
   153	          ],
   154	        },
   155	      ];
   156	
   157	      // Replay: collect all events
   158	      const allNodes: Array<{ kind: string; id: string }> = [];
   159	      const allEdges: Array<{ kind: string; source: string; target: string }> = [];
   160	
   161	      for (const record of iterationRecords) {
   162	        for (const event of record.graphEvents) {
   163	          if (event.kind === 'node') allNodes.push(event as { kind: string; id: string });
   164	          if (event.kind === 'edge') allEdges.push(event as { kind: string; source: string; target: string });
   165	        }
   166	      }
   167	
   168	      expect(allNodes.length).toBe(2);
   169	      expect(allEdges.length).toBe(2);
   170	    });
   171	
   172	    it('replay produces deterministic results', () => {
   173	      const events = [
   174	        { kind: 'node', id: 'a', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'A' },
   175	        { kind: 'node', id: 'b', nodeType: 'finding_node', label: 'B' },
   176	        { kind: 'edge', source: 'a', target: 'b', relation: 'CITES' },
   177	      ];
   178	
   179	      // Two replays of the same events should produce the same graph
   180	      const replay1Nodes = events.filter((e) => e.kind === 'node');
   181	      const replay2Nodes = events.filter((e) => e.kind === 'node');
   182	
   183	      expect(replay1Nodes.length).toBe(replay2Nodes.length);
   184	      expect(replay1Nodes.map((n) => n.id)).toEqual(replay2Nodes.map((n) => n.id));
   185	    });
   186	  });
   187	
   188	  describe('namespace scoping', () => {
   189	    it('namespaces isolate graph data between sessions', () => {
   190	      const ns1 = 'dr-session-1';
   191	      const ns2 = 'dr-session-2';
   192	      expect(ns1).not.toBe(ns2);
   193	      // Contract: nodes in ns1 are invisible from ns2 queries
   194	    });
   195	
   196	    it('namespace matches sessionId format', () => {
   197	      const namespace = 'dr-2026-03-18T10-00-00Z';
   198	      expect(namespace).toMatch(/^(dr|rvw)-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T/);
   199	    });
   200	  });
   201	
   202	  describe('node type taxonomy', () => {
   203	    it('research node types are well-defined', () => {
   204	      const researchNodeTypes = ['question_node', 'finding_node', 'claim_node', 'source_node'];
   205	      expect(researchNodeTypes.length).toBe(4);
   206	      for (const type of researchNodeTypes) {
   207	        expect(type).toMatch(/_node$/);
   208	      }
   209	    });
   210	
   211	    it('review node types are well-defined', () => {
   212	      const reviewNodeTypes = ['dimension_node', 'file_node', 'finding_node', 'evidence_node'];
   213	      expect(reviewNodeTypes.length).toBe(4);
   214	      for (const type of reviewNodeTypes) {
   215	        expect(type).toMatch(/_node$/);
   216	      }
   217	    });
   218	  });
   219	
   220	  describe('edge relation taxonomy', () => {
   221	    it('research relations are well-defined', () => {
   222	      const researchRelations = ['ANSWERS', 'CONTRADICTS', 'CITES', 'EXTENDS', 'SUPERSEDES'];
   223	      expect(researchRelations.length).toBe(5);
   224	      for (const rel of researchRelations) {
   225	        expect(rel).toBe(rel.toUpperCase());
   226	      }
   227	    });
   228	
   229	    it('review relations are well-defined', () => {
   230	      const reviewRelations = ['COVERS', 'CONTRADICTS', 'EVIDENCES', 'REMEDIATES', 'DEPENDS_ON'];
   231	      expect(reviewRelations.length).toBe(5);
   232	      for (const rel of reviewRelations) {

all listed research relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; research metrics use the defined formulas. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:342:| REQ-004 | Review coverage graphs MUST use the defined node kinds, relation types, initial weight estimates, and metadata contract. | `DIMENSION`, `FILE`, `FINDING`, `EVIDENCE`, and `REMEDIATION` are canonical; all listed review relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; review metrics use the defined formulas. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:353:| REQ-010 | Human-readable graph status summaries MUST be part of the contract, not a debugging afterthought. | `deep_loop_graph_status` returns documented counts and signal summaries suitable for dashboards and synthesis surfaces; standalone visualization is deferred to a later phase. |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:361:- **SC-001**: The phase documents the coverage graph as the fourth graph system in the Spec Kit Memory stack and clearly reuses existing graph patterns.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:455:- As a maintainer of deep review, I want coverage graph queries that show which files, dimensions, and findings still need evidence so reducer decisions stay explainable.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict. }));\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5550:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-cross-layer.vitest.ts:373:    upsertNode(makeReviewNode(specFolder, sessionId, 'review-finding', 'FINDING', 'Review finding'));\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5551:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-cross-layer.vitest.ts:378:      sessionId,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5574:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5581:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5584:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5586:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5588:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5596:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5600:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5641:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5647:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5681:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5688:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5691:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5693:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5695:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5703:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5707:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5748:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5754:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:3:description: \\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\"\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:71:1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:72:2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:75:5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:114:| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:90:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"D5: Does the coverage graph's contribution to the 3-signal convergence math exceed its weight, or is it merely nominal?\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:301:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`sk-improve-agent` follows a different path entirely: its resume contract replays a local \\\\\\\"coverage graph + registry\\\\\\\", and `mutation-coverage.cjs` persists an `improvement` JSON graph with `fs`/`path` helpers and `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the agent only consumes an orchestrator-provided summary as advisory context. That means it does not supply the missing shared `deep_loop_graph_*` bridge for research/review convergence (.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:9-10; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:50-67; .opencode/agent/agent-improver.md:175-177).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:319:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"Both deep-loop convergence contracts still document a reducer-owned in-memory graph bridge that is not present in the live reducer: deep research says \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph\\\\\\\" when `graphEvents` exist, and deep review says the same for review iterations (.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205; .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:658-683).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:711:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published deep-research contract says that when `graphEvents` exist, \\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph and derives additional signals for the legal-stop gate evaluation,\\\\\\\" including graph coverage sub-checks (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205`). The live research reducer does not implement that bridge: its only imports are `fs` and `path`, and its convergence score is taken from `latestIteration.convergenceSignals?.compositeStop` or `latestIteration?.newInfoRatio` rather than any graph helper (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-13`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:244-249`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1530:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2121:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"`SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2513:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"I did not find a shipped orchestrator implementation file that performs the promised “replay journal + coverage graph + registry before dispatch,” so this pass had to prove absence via reducer inputs and helper interfaces rather than a single end-to-end resume driver.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2558:      \\\"text\\\": \\\"A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\\",\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:130:### Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: The review reducer/dashboard still show only `sessionId`, `lineageMode`, and `generation`, but without a live restart/fork/completed-continue write path this stayed downstream of F010 rather than a distinct release-level defect. -- BLOCKED (iteration 5, 1 retry: Dashboard-only ancestry surfacing: The review reducer/dashboard still show only `sessionId`, `lineageMode`, and `generation`, but without a live restart/fork/completed-continue write path this defect.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:160:### Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The attempts)\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:161:- What was tried: Handler fallback without `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:163:- `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:185:### handling.\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:195:### Looking for a second handler-side auth or validation bypass in `mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts`: the handler validates `specFolder`, `loopType`, `sessionId`, relation/kind enums, and self-loops, so this pass did not uncover a
